prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `2037f2c7`.
codex_response:
Rule: find the two nonzero multicolor objects, ignore color, normalize them to a shared top-left origin, take the symmetric difference of their occupied cells, then crop that difference and paint it in color `8`.

The `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint was used; it matched the official examples exactly. No new reusable primitive was needed. I implemented three task-local glyph families in the generator so the inputs stay close to the originals: two tall stacked multicolor objects on black, sharing a color scheme within each pair, with one object differing only by a carved contiguous row band that yields the same 2/3/4-row cropped XOR outputs seen in the source task. Official verification passed `4/4`, generated verification passed `1000/1000`, and I visually checked a preview sheet.
